meaning: I would be grateful / I would appreciate
communicative_purpose: To make polite requests while expressing gratitude in advance
trigger_situations: Formal requests in emails, letters, professional communications
contexts: Formal written communication, business correspondence, administrative contexts
variations: je vous serais obligé, je vous serais très obligé, je vous saurais gré
common_mistakes: Agreement errors with 'reconnaissant'; confusing conditional tense
example_1: Je vous serais reconnaissant de bien vouloir répondre avant le 15.
example_2: Je vous serais reconnaissant de m'envoyer les documents par email.
example_3: Je vous serais très reconnaissant si vous pouviez me recevoir.
example_1_translation: I would be grateful if you could respond before the 15th.
example_2_translation: I would be grateful if you could send me the documents by email.
example_3_translation: I would be very grateful if you could receive me.

meaning: I keep myself informed / I stay informed
communicative_purpose: To express that one follows developments or stays updated on a topic
trigger_situations: Professional updates, industry news, project monitoring
contexts: Professional contexts, business correspondence, workplace discussions
variations: je reste informé, je m'informe régulièrement, je tiens à jour
common_mistakes: Agreement errors with 'se tenir'; confusing with passive constructions
example_1: Je me tiens informé des dernières évolutions du marché.
example_2: Je me tiens informé des avancées de votre projet.
example_3: Nous nous tenons informés des changements réglementaires.
example_1_translation: I keep myself informed of the latest market developments.
example_2_translation: I will keep you informed of the progress of your project.
example_3_translation: We stay informed of regulatory changes.

meaning: it should be noted that / note that
communicative_purpose: To draw attention to important information or facts
trigger_situations: Reports, formal presentations, academic writing, official documents
variations: il convient de noter que, notons que, il faut noter que, il importe de noter
common_mistakes: Using in casual contexts; confusing impersonal construction
example_1: Il est à noter que les ventes ont augmenté de 20%.
example_2: Il est à noter que ce phénomène touche principalement les jeunes.
example_3: Il est à noter que cette règle ne s'applique qu'aux contrats signés après 2020.
example_1_translation: It should be noted that sales increased by 20%.
example_2_translation: It should be noted that this phenomenon mainly affects young people.
example_3_translation: Please note that this rule only applies to contracts signed after 2020.

meaning: I am sharing with you / I am informing you of
communicative_purpose: To officially communicate or share information, decisions, or opinions
trigger_situations: Official communications, company announcements, formal notifications
variations: je me permets de vous faire part, je vous informe de, je porte à votre connaissance
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'partager' (to share); using wrong preposition 'de'
example_1: Je vous fais part de notre décision concernant le projet.
example_2: Je vous fais part de ma satisfaction quant aux résultats obtenus.
example_3: Je vous fais part de quelques observations importantes.
example_1_translation: I am informing you of our decision regarding the project.
example_2_translation: I express my satisfaction with the results obtained.
example_3_translation: I share with you some important observations.

communicative_purpose: To indicate that something is considered or understood based on new information
contexts: Formal written/spoken, analysis, reports, professional discussions
variations: compte tenu de, étant donné, au vu de, eu égard à
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'à cause de' (because of); incorrect preposition usage
example_1: À la lumière de ces nouveaux éléments, nous devons reconsidérer notre position.
example_2: À la lumière des événements récents, la situation a changé.
example_3: À la lumière de votre demande, nous avons adapté notre offre.
example_1_translation: In light of these new elements, we must reconsider our position.
example_2_translation: In light of recent events, the situation has changed.
example_3_translation: In light of your request, we have adapted our offer.

meaning: I bring to your attention / I inform you of
communicative_purpose: To officially inform someone about something important
trigger_situations: Official notifications, legal communications, administrative procedures
contexts: Formal written, legal/administrative contexts, official correspondence
variations: je me permets de porter à votre connaissance, j'attire votre attention sur
common_mistakes: Using too frequently in casual emails; wrong preposition 'de' after 'connaissance'
example_1: Je porte à votre connaissance que la réunion est reportée.
example_2: Je porte à votre connaissance un changement important dans la réglementation.
example_3: Je porte à votre connaissance les résultats de l'audit annuel.
example_1_translation: I would like to inform you that the meeting has been postponed.
example_2_translation: I am bringing to your attention an important change in the regulations.
example_3_translation: I bring to your attention the results of the annual audit.

meaning: it is my pleasure to / I am pleased to
communicative_purpose: Polite way to introduce positive news or pleasant information
trigger_situations: Acceptances, congratulations, positive announcements in formal letters
variations: c'est avec plaisir que, j'ai le plaisir de, il me fait plaisir de
common_mistakes: Using in negative contexts; overusing in casual correspondence
example_1: Il m'est agréable de vous informer de votre promotion.
example_2: Il m'est agréable de vous confirmer notre collaboration.
example_3: Il nous est agréable de vous accueillir parmi nous.
example_1_translation: It is my pleasure to inform you of your promotion.
example_2_translation: It is a pleasure for me to confirm our collaboration.
example_3_translation: It is our pleasure to welcome you among us.

meaning: I am still awaiting / I continue to wait
communicative_purpose: Polite way to remind of outstanding request or pending response
trigger_situations: Follow-up emails, polite reminders, pending administrative matters
variations: je suis toujours dans l'attente, dans l'attente de votre retour, dans l'attente de vous lire
common_mistakes: Using in aggressive or impatient tone; forgetting preposition 'de'
example_1: Je reste dans l'attente de votre réponse concernant ma candidature.
example_2: Je reste dans l'attente de votre confirmation pour finaliser le dossier.
example_3: Nous restons dans l'attente de votre decision finale.
example_1_translation: I am waiting for your response regarding my application.
example_2_translation: I am waiting for your confirmation to finalize the file.
example_3_translation: We remain awaiting your final decision.

meaning: in my capacity as / by virtue of my position
communicative_purpose: To specify the official position from which one speaks or acts
trigger_situations: Official communications, formal declarations, institutional contexts
contexts: Formal written/spoken, institutional contexts, legal documents
variations: en ma qualité professionnelle, en tant que, en qualité de
common_mistakes: Confusing gender; using in informal contexts where title is irrelevant
example_1: En ma qualité de directeur, je vous informe de cette décision.
example_2: En ma qualité de représentant légal, je signe ce contrat.
example_3: En sa qualité de médecin traitant, elle a accès à ces informations.
example_1_translation: In my capacity as director, I inform you of this decision.
example_2_translation: In my capacity as legal representative, I sign this contract.
example_3_translation: As a treating physician, she has access to this information.

meaning: I seize this opportunity to / I take advantage of this occasion to
communicative_purpose: To use an occasion to bring up a topic or make a request
trigger_situations: Letter openings after pleasantries, business communications, formal greetings
variations: je profite de cette occasion pour, je me permets de saisir cette occasion pour
example_1: Je saisis cette occasion pour vous remercier de votre accueil chaleureux.
example_2: Je saisis cette occasion pour vous présenter nos meilleurs vœux.
example_3: Je saisis cette occasion pour vous rappeler l'importance du délai.
example_1_translation: I would like to take this opportunity to thank you for your warm welcome.
example_2_translation: I would like to take this opportunity to extend our best wishes to you.
example_3_translation: I take this opportunity to remind you of the importance of the deadline.

chunk_text: je me vois dans l'obligation de
meaning: I find myself obligated to / I feel compelled to
communicative_purpose: To express that circumstances require an action, often reluctantly
trigger_situations: Formal complaints, warnings, formal notices, disciplinary contexts
contexts: Formal written communication, legal contexts, HR communications
variations: je me vois dans la nécessité de, je me vois dans l'impossibilité de, il m'est nécessaire de
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'se voir offrir' (to be offered); wrong preposition 'de'
patterns: je + me + vois + dans + l'obligation + de + [action]
example_1: Je me vois dans l'obligation de vous rappeler vos engagements contractuels.
example_2: Je me vois dans l'obligation de signaler un retard dans le planning.
example_3: Je me vois dans l'obligation de mettre fin à notre collaboration.
example_1_translation: I see myself obliged to remind you of your contractual commitments.
example_2_translation: I see myself obliged to report a delay in the schedule.
example_3_translation: I see myself obliged to end our collaboration.

meaning: I fully endorse / I fully agree with
communicative_purpose: To express strong approval or agreement with a proposal, idea, or position
trigger_situations: Formal agreements, endorsements, support for proposals, collaborative contexts
contexts: Formal written/spoken, professional discussions, formal agreements
variations: je souscris entièrement à, je m'associe à, je suis en accord avec
common_mistakes: Confusing with 'souscrire à un emprunt' (to subscribe to a loan); preposition 'à' required
example_1: Je souscris pleinement à cette proposition de collaboration.
example_2: Je souscris pleinement à votre analyse de la situation.
example_3: Nous souscrivons pleinement aux objectifs fixés par le groupe.
example_1_translation: I fully subscribe to this proposal for collaboration.
example_2_translation: I fully subscribe to your analysis of the situation.
example_3_translation: We fully subscribe to the objectives set by the group.

meaning: I am setting forth / I am presenting to you
communicative_purpose: To present or explain something formally, especially problems or situations
trigger_situations: Formal letters explaining issues, business proposals, formal reports
contexts: Formal written communication, legal contexts, business correspondence
variations: je me permets de vous exposer, je tiens à vous exposer, je porte à votre connaissance
common_mistakes: Using in spoken contexts; confusing with 'exposer' (to expose physically)
example_1: Je vous expose ci-dessous les difficultés rencontrées.
example_2: Je vous expose ma situation afin que vous compreniez mes contraintes.
example_3: Je vous expose les faits tels qu'ils se sont produits.
example_1_translation: Below I outline the difficulties encountered.
example_2_translation: I will explain my situation to you so that you understand my constraints.
example_3_translation: I am telling you the facts as they happened.

meaning: with regard to / in comparison with / in view of
communicative_purpose: To compare or relate something to another thing or standard
trigger_situations: Analysis, comparisons, formal reports, professional assessments
contexts: Formal written, analysis, reports, legal contexts
variations: par rapport à, en comparaison de, vis-à-vis de, au regard de
example_1: En regard de ces résultats, nous devons ajuster notre stratégie.
example_2: En regard des normes établies, ce résultat est satisfaisant.
example_3: En regard du budget initial, nous avons respecté les coûts.
example_1_translation: Given these results, we must adjust our strategy.
example_2_translation: Compared to established standards, this result is satisfactory.
example_3_translation: Considering the initial budget, we respected the costs.

meaning: further to / with reference to / in response to
communicative_purpose: To formally refer to a previous letter, email, or conversation
trigger_situations: Reply letters, follow-up communications, formal correspondence chains
variations: en réponse à, suite à, faisant suite à, en référence à
example_1: Je fais suite à votre courrier du 15 mars dernier.
example_2: Je fais suite à notre entretien téléphonique de ce matin.
example_3: Je fais suite à votre email dans lequel vous demandiez des précisions.
example_1_translation: I am following up on your letter of March 15.
example_2_translation: I follow up on our telephone interview this morning.
example_3_translation: I am following up on your email in which you asked for details.
